params.text:
Systems that keep you safe

A safe system of work is a set of agreed steps that make sure someone always knows where you are and can act if you do not check in. These are practical habits as much as procedures, and they only work if everyone uses them every time.

  • Check-in and check-out: let a nominated person know when you arrive and when you leave, so a missed call triggers a response.
  • Buddy systems: pair up so a colleague knows your movements and can raise the alarm.
  • Lone-worker devices and apps: alarms that can send an alert and your location at the press of a button.
  • Share your schedule: make sure someone has your visit list and expected timings.
  • Keep a charged phone: a working, charged phone is your simplest lifeline. Never set off without one.

params.alt: Illustration for Safe systems of work

params.taskDescription: Drag each word into the gap to complete these safe-working habits.
params.textField: Always follow your *check-in* procedure so someone knows you are safe. Carry a fully *charged* phone as your simplest lifeline. Share your *schedule* so a colleague knows where you should be. Press your lone-worker *alarm* if you feel threatened. When you finish a visit, remember to *check-out* so a missed call raises concern.
